title: sidalcea calycosa
intro: Sidalcea calycosa, also known as the mountain molly, is a perennial wildflower that grows in the mountainous regions of the western United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its high concentration of salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin, which has been shown to provide relief from pain and reduce inflammation. In herbal preparations, Sidalcea calycosa can be made into a tea, tincture, or salve to take advantage of its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing.
